sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 7.48 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.7)

statement.latex: (Well-known problem). Suppose that, in a finite group$G$, each two elements of the same order are conjugate. Is then$|G| 6$? { S.\,A.\,Syskin} Yes, it is, mod CFSG (P.\,Fitzpatrick, Proc. Roy. Irish Acad. Sect. A, { 85}, no.\,1 (1985), 53--58); see also (W.\,Feit, G.\,Seitz, Illinois J. Math., { 33}, no.\,1 (1988), 103--131) and (R.\,W.\,van~der~Waall, A.\,Bensa\"{ }d, Simon Stevin, { 65} (1991), ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
